IMPORTANT: Comments are subject to validation constraints enforced by the MCP server - maximum 65536 characters for the complete comment (including footer which is added automatically), 10 mentions (@username), and 50 links. Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. If your GitHub App lacks Discussions permission, set 'discussions: false' in the workflow's safe-outputs.add-comment configuration to exclude this permission. If these limits are exceeded, the tool call will fail with a detailed error message indicating which constraint was violated.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. Auto-targeting only works for issue, pull_request, discussion, and comment event triggers — it does NOT work for schedule, workflow_dispatch, push, or workflow_run triggers. For those trigger types, always provide item_number explicitly, or the comment will be silently discarded.","type":"number"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"}},{"name":"add_labels","description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"}},{"name":"noop","description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"}},{"name":"missing_data","description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"}}]}}

It is a sub-issue of the parent tracking issue [#53864 – Azure Mgmt Generator migration](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/53864).\n\n**Status**: Reopened – migration is not yet complete (per assignee comment).  \n**Assignee**: @live1206\n\n---\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📋 Context \u0026 Background\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- The SDK at `sdk/storage/Azure.ResourceManager.Storage` currently uses AutoRest (`autorest.md` present, no `tsp-location.yaml`).\n- The migration involves adopting TypeSpec-driven generation via the Azure Management Generator (`eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt`).\n- Parent tracking issue #53864 documents completed RPs at [#55080](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/55080).\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e✅ Migration Checklist (typical sub-tasks)\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [ ] Locate the TypeSpec source for the Storage RP (likely in `azure-rest-api-specs` or `azure-rest-api-specs-pr`)\n- [ ] Create `tsp-location.yaml` in `sdk/storage/Azure.ResourceManager.Storage`\n- [ ] Run TypeSpec generation via `tsp-client update` and validate output\n- [ ] Apply any necessary SDK-side customizations (partial classes, `CodeGenType`, `CodeGenSuppress`) to preserve existing API surface\n- [ ] Apply TypeSpec-side decorators (`@@clientName`, `@@access`, `@@alternateType`) as needed to resolve breaking changes\n- [ ] Run pre-commit checks: `dotnet format`, export public API (`eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 storage`), update snippets\n- [ ] Run unit tests and verify no regressions\n- [ ] Ensure changelog is updated\n- [ ] Submit PR for review\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🛠️ Debugging / Migration Tips\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- Use the `sdk-migration` Copilot skill for migration guidance specific to this repo.\n- Use the `mitigate-breaking-changes` Copilot skill for patterns on preserving API compatibility.\n- Check [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) and the MPG [migration skill](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/.github/copilot-instructions.md) for workflow details.\n- Storage has many sub-resources (BlobService, FileService, QueueService, TableService, StorageAccount, etc.) — expect a large generated surface. Plan for incremental review.\n- If sync methods are missing `using DiagnosticScope scope = ...`, this is a known emitter bug in some Collection/Resource files — check and add customizations as needed.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📚 Useful Resources\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [Local SDK Workflow Instructions](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/common/instructions/azsdk-tools/local-sdk-workflow.instructions.md)\n- [MPG Migration PR Review skill](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/.github/copilot-instructions.md)\n- [Breaking Change Mitigation patterns](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/common/instructions/copilot/)\n- [Azure ResourceManager.Storage SDK](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/storage/Azure.ResourceManager.Storage)\n- [Parent tracking issue #53864](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/53864)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n","item_number":54775},"name":"add_comment"}}}
